message:
📖 docs(leaderboard): explain why Social click counts may look stuck (#1499)

Add a collapsible 'Why is my Social count not updating?' block below
the existing 'Earn Social Clicks' section on the leaderboard page. Covers
four real reasons contributors see counts that don't match what they
shared, so maintainers stop fielding the same Slack question.

Reasons documented:
1. GA4 attribution dimensions (utm_campaign/utm_term) have a 24-48h
   processing lag — same-day shares appear the next day.
2. Chat apps (WhatsApp, Discord, Messenger) strip the ?utm_*= query
   string when generating link previews; recipients land as
   direct/(not set) and can't be credited back.
3. Sessions, not page views — same recipient clicking repeatedly in
   a 30-min window = one session.
4. Legacy intern-0X still works, but new shares should use
   utm_term=github-handle so credit rolls up under GitHub identity.

Paired with kubestellar/console PR #9153 which fixes a silent-drop in
the affiliate-clicks Netlify function for GitHub-login utm_terms
sampled under the legacy intern_outreach campaign.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -559,6 +559,35 @@ export default function LeaderboardPage() { utm_term = your GitHub handle, lowercase utm_medium = twitter, linkedin, blog, youtube, devto, etc. + + {/* Why counts may look "stuck" — reduce support churn */} +
+ + Why is my Social count not updating? + +
    +
  • + Google Analytics attribution lag: The + utm_campaign / + utm_term dimensions take + 24–48 hours to finalize after a click. + Same-day shares typically appear here the next day. +
    • +
  • + Chat apps strip UTM tags: WhatsApp, Discord, Messenger, and some LinkedIn preview renderers strip the + ?utm_*=… query string when generating link previews. Recipients who click the previewed + (un-tagged) link land as direct / (not set) traffic and cannot be credited back to you. Prefer plain-text shares via email, SMS, GitHub comments, or blog posts where the query string is preserved. +
    • +
  • + Sessions, not page views: One recipient clicking your link multiple times within a 30-minute window counts as a single session. To grow the count, share to + more people, not the same people more often. +
    • +
  • + Legacy intern-0X links keep working, but new shares should use + utm_term=your-github-handle so credits roll up under your GitHub identity rather than a numbered slot. +
    • +
+
)}
